stem: A electronics testing study measures the battery life (hours) for a sample of 50 smartphone models. The sample mean is 421.1 hours with a sample standard deviation of 88.3 hours. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 421.1 hours. 95% CI = [396.0, 446.2]. Point estimate: 421.1 hours.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
